description_long: This is a single, fully bloomed rose flower with large, overlapping petals that display a gradient of colors from creamy white at the base to soft pink and deeper rose hues at the edges. The petals are arranged in a tight, spiral whorl at the center, gradually opening outward in layered, concentric circles. The outer petals are slightly curled and have subtle, natural imperfections along their edges, giving the flower a lifelike appearance. The flower's base is visible, showing a short section of the stem and some dried sepal remnants. There are no visible leaves attached to the flower, and the overall form is rounded and symmetrical. The coloration and petal structure suggest a cultivated rose, commonly found in urban gardens, parks, or as decorative elements in public spaces.
description: A fully bloomed rose with creamy white and pink petals arranged in a layered, spiral whorl, showing a natural, rounded form and subtle color gradients.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the symmetrical spiral arrangement of the petals and the full bloom of the rose.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the layered petals and the depth of the flower, with the inner whorl partially visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the outer petals curling outward and the short stem base, emphasizing the flower's rounded profile.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the opposite direction, showing overlapping petals and the flower's natural asymmetry.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral whorl, with overlapping layers radiating outward from a central point. The arrangement is radial and symmetrical, typical of cultivated roses, with each petal partially covering the one beneath it.
